summary: Postinstall script harvests developer identity from git/gh credential stores and exfiltrates to opaque Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js performs an unattended identity-harvest of the installing developer. It reads installer-owned identity and credential-bearing files outside the package's own state — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI host file that stores oauth_token) — to extract the developer's git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider, and environment variables EMAIL / GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL. The complete payload, including raw plaintext scmEmail/githubLogin/githubEmail/hostname/username/cwd, is POSTed at install time to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The script header contains a cover-story comment claiming `data is fully anonymous... a one-way hash of your machine identifier. No source code, tokens, or credentials are ever transmitted` — this is contradicted by the payload, which transmits raw identity fields with no hashing, and by the destination, which is an unbranded `npm-package-logger-*.run.app` Cloud Run host rather than the vendor-branded `ollama-js.dev` endpoint named in the README's telemetry policy. The deceptive cover comment, the targeting of GitHub CLI credential-store paths, the breadth of identity sources scraped, and the opaque destination together rule out a legitimate telemetry interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
